subject: Computer Networking
title: How Routers Find the Best Path
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on How Routers Find the Best Path.

hook.text: A packet leaves your laptop. Between you and the server are dozens of routers. Which path does it take?

buildup.text: Routers maintain tables listing known networks and the best next hop toward each destination.

discovery.text: Routing protocols like BGP and OSPF share path info between routers so tables stay up to date.

twist.text: The best path can change in seconds — a failed link triggers routers to recalculate instantly.

climax.text: Your packet might cross ten routers in ten countries. Each one decides the next hop independently.

punchline.text: Routers don't know the full path. They just know the next step.

quiz.question: How do routers decide where to send a packet?
options: They consult a routing table to find the best next hop
They broadcast the packet to every device on the internet
They wait for the destination to pull the data
